title: Manufacturing Equipment Engineer Trainee
description:
Position Summary:

As a Graduate Trainee in Manufacturing Equipment Engineering, you will gain hands-on experience in smart manufacturing within a high-volume production environment. You’ll support the maintenance and optimization of optical, mechanical, electrical, and fluidics systems to enhance equipment reliability and uptime. The role involves applying engineering principles to troubleshoot issues, drive continuous improvement, and assist in the adoption of the new SAP ERP system.


Position Responsibilities:

Operational Excellence & Compliance

  • Complete all required training (e.g., cGMP, safety, work instructions) within the stipulated timeframe.
  • Perform assigned tasks in full compliance with cGMP, safety, quality, and work instruction requirements.
  • Uphold safety protocols, quality standards, and company policies at all times.
  • Maintain high standards of workplace organization and cleanliness through 6S practices.
  • Report and escalate any work-related or personal concerns promptly to supervisors.
  • Meet daily production schedules and performance objectives.
  • Support the team by taking on additional responsibilities or ad hoc tasks when required.

Equipment Maintenance & Reliability

  • Perform preventive maintenance activities and assist in less routine maintenance tasks under supervision.
  • Execute repair and recovery procedures to restore equipment and system functionality.
  • Learn and optimize equipment asset data within the ERP system to improve data accuracy and traceability.
  • Identify and update critical spare parts in SAP to ensure operational continuity.

Continuous Improvement

  • Contribute to equipment performance improvement and reliability enhancement initiatives.
  • Support and promote continuous improvement programs such as 6S and Lean practices.
  • Review and update work instructions and maintenance documentation to ensure consistent execution and performance.

Incident Investigation & Reporting

  • Participate in incident investigations, contributing to data gathering, analysis, and report preparation.
  • Support team members during investigations and corrective action implementation.
  • Apply sound technical writing techniques to produce clear, concise, and accurate documentation.


Learning & Technical Development

  • Develop hands-on competency in troubleshooting, repairing, and maintaining automation and PLC systems.
  • Assist technicians and engineers in resolving chronic or recurring equipment issues.
  • Support improvements to existing maintenance processes for better efficiency and reliability.
  • Participate in the design and optimization of automation equipment to enhance product quality, cycle time, and cost efficiency.

Note: Listed responsibilities are essential but not exhaustive. Duties may evolve based on business needs.


Position Requirements

  • Strong understanding of electrical, electronics, mechanical, and pneumatic diagrams.
  • Commitment to compliance with cGMP, quality, and safety practices.
  • Ability to support the transition from manual to automated processes.
  • Willingness to learn and contribute to ERP system adoption.
  • Gain experience in troubleshooting PLCs (e.g., Beckhoff, Allen Bradley).
  • Ability to complete tasks safely and efficiently.
  • High level of discipline, integrity, and attention to detail.
  • Capacity to manage multiple tasks effectively.
  • Ability to work in noisy and chemical environments with appropriate PPE.
  • Visual acuity to differentiate colors for component and chemical identification.
  • Positive attitude, sense of urgency, and organizational skills.
  • Physical ability to safely lift weights up to 15kg.

All listed requirements are considered essential. Reasonable accommodations may be made based on business conditions.


Preferred Experience/Education/Skills:

  • Bachelor’s Degree in Electrical, Electronics, Mechanical or Mechatronics will have added advantage.

postedCompany.description:
ILLUMINA SINGAPORE PTE. LTD.

At Illumina, our goal is to apply innovative technologies to the analysis of genetic variation and function, making studies possible that were not even imaginable just a few years ago. It is mission critical for us to deliver innovative, flexible, and scalable solutions to meet the needs of our customers. As a global company, we strive to meet this challenge – we do this through placing high value on collaborative interactions, offering rapid delivery of solutions, and providing the highest level of quality.  Illumina's innovative sequencing and array technologies are fueling groundbreaking advancements in life science research, translational and consumer genomics, and molecular diagnostics.
